summary: Iran's internet connectivity partially resumed after 88 days of shutdown. Israeli forces reported expanded ground attacks in southern Lebanon and targeted Hamas leadership in Gaza. Lebanese health officials said Israeli airstrikes killed 31 and injured 40 in the past 24 hours.
key_points: Partial restoration of internet in Iran after 88 days of near-total isolation (Source: @shin_persian/6239).
U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio discussed negotiations in Qatar regarding Iran (Source: @mamlekate/103585).
Iranian officials emphasized protecting national sovereignty while extending support to Islamic brotherly states (Source: @naya_foriraq/76167).
Lebanon's Health Ministry reported 31 killed and 40 injured by Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon over 24 hours (Source: @presstv/191515).
Israeli military expanded ground attacks in southern Lebanon and conducted raids north of the Litani River (Source: @presstv/191513).
Israeli internal security minister warned against a Trump‑Iran agreement (Source: @kianmeli1/87671).
Iranian IRGC boats were identified approaching commercial shipping and possibly laying mines in the Persian Gulf (Source: @shin_persian/6236).

sections.executive_summary: Partial internet restoration in Iran after 88 days of shutdown marks a shift in domestic communications. Israeli forces intensified operations in southern Lebanon, causing significant casualties. Hamas leadership in Gaza remains a focal target for Israeli strikes.

points: Live metrics confirmed a partial restoration of internet connectivity in Iran on day 88 after 2093 hours of near‑total isolation, the longest nationwide shutdown in modern history (Source: @shin_persian/6239).
U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio indicated ongoing talks in Qatar about possible progress on Iran negotiations during his official visit to India (Source: @mamlekate/103585).
Iranian officials reiterated commitment to protect national sovereignty while extending support to Islamic brotherly nations (Source: @naya_foriraq/76167).
IRGC boats were identified in the Persian Gulf approaching commercial vessels and possibly attempting to lay mines, according to CENTCOM‑derived reports (Source: @shin_persian/6236).
Israel expanded ground attacks in southern Lebanon, stepping up air and ground operations across the region, including raids north of the Litani River (Source: @presstv/191513).
Israeli internal security minister warned that the United States must not allow former President Trump to sign a bad deal with Iran (Source: @kianmeli1/87671).
Israeli forces reported targeting Hamas commander Muhammad Oda in Gaza, describing him as a key architect of the Oct 7 massacre (Source: @inon_yttach/8338).
The IDF spokesperson stated continued strikes on Hezbollah terrorist infrastructure in southern Lebanon, eliminating militants and targeting launch sites (Source: @idf_telegram/23887).
Israeli forces announced an ongoing operation in Gaza targeting Muhammad Oda, a senior Hamas intelligence chief appointed after the killing of Az Eddin al‑Haddad (Source: @inon_yttach/8338).
The Israeli Defense Forces reported neutralizing a terrorist infiltrator who threw stones at a camp in Jenin, resulting in no Israeli casualties (Source: @New_security8200/199964).
Lebanon's Health Ministry reported 31 deaths and 40 injuries from Israeli airstrikes across southern Lebanon in the last 24 hours (Source: @presstv/191515).
IDF statements confirmed continued strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon, eliminating militants and targeting rocket launch facilities (Source: @idf_telegram/23887).
Hezbollah‑linked sources listed multiple armored assets (e.g., Merkhava tanks, Hamr vehicles) operating in the Zawtar eastern sector and near the Banat Jbeil area (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/361805).
Israeli media noted intensive movements of armored personnel carriers and medical evacuation teams in southern Lebanon (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/361827).
Heavy combat aircraft were reported intruding near Netua Tzofar, triggering a Red Alert on 26 May 2026 (Source: @tzevaadom_en/67484).
A tanker reported an external explosion on its port side aft, with some bunker fuel leaking, though crew remained safe (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3047).

Iranian negotiators traveled to Doha to discuss the release of frozen Iranian assets worth $24 billion, seeking immediate release of half upon a memorandum of understanding with the U.S. (Source: @asafroz/49526).
Iranian officials returned from Doha after discussions focused on unblocking Iranian funds (Source: @VahidOnline/75737).

key_developments: Partial internet connectivity resumed in Iran after 88 days of near‑total shutdown, marking the longest nationwide isolation in modern history (Source: @shin_persian/6239).
Lebanese Health Ministry confirmed 31 fatalities and 40 injuries from Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon within 24 hours (Source: @presstv/191515).
Israeli military expanded ground operations in southern Lebanon and conducted raids north of the Litani River (Source: @presstv/191513).
U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio reported ongoing negotiations in Qatar concerning Iran (Source: @mamlekate/103585).
IRGC vessels were detected in the Persian Gulf approaching commercial shipping and possibly laying mines (Source: @shin_persian/6236).
Israeli forces targeted Hamas commander Muhammad Oda in Gaza, labeling him a chief architect of the Oct 7 attacks (Source: @inon_yttach/8338).
Hezbollah‑linked reports listed deployment of Merkhava tanks, Hamr vehicles, and other armored assets in eastern Zawtar and near Banat Jbeil, Lebanon (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/361805).
sections.threat_assessment: Iran faces internal pressure as internet services only partially resume after an unprecedented shutdown, while external diplomatic efforts continue in Qatar. Israel's intensified operations in southern Lebanon and Gaza increase the risk of broader regional escalation, especially given Hezbollah's reported armored deployments. The presence of IRGC boats near commercial routes in the Persian Gulf adds a maritime security threat that could affect international shipping.
sections.regional_response: Lebanese authorities have reported significant civilian casualties from Israeli strikes, prompting heightened alert levels and potential mobilization of local militias. Iran's negotiation team in Doha seeks to unlock billions in frozen assets, indicating a strategic push to alleviate economic pressure. The United States, through Secretary Rubio, appears engaged in diplomatic channels, though Israeli officials warn against any U.S. agreement that could favor Iran.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of IRGC maritime activity in the Persian Gulf is critical to prevent potential mining of commercial lanes. Continued surveillance of Hezbollah's armored movements in eastern Lebanon is essential for anticipating future ground engagements. Israeli targeting of Hamas leadership in Gaza suggests a focus on decapitating command structures, which may lead to intensified urban combat and collateral damage.
